 {"id":"bd-u4fgt","title":"Scale Adaptation + Fidelity Tiers","description":"Background
- Diagrams must scale with available screen space.

Scope
- Compute world layout bounds and fit to terminal area.
- Fidelity tiers (compact/normal/rich) driven by area + density thresholds.
- Rules for padding expansion, decoration, and label truncation/hiding.
- Monotone rounding for stable cell placement.
- Tier-specific reflow: compact = simplified routing/labels; rich = expanded spacing and decoration.
- Reserve regions for auxiliary panels (link footnotes, legends) based on config.

Considerations
- Deterministic tier selection; log tier and thresholds.
- Preserve readability at 80x24; enhance richness at large sizes (up to 6K widths).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for tier selection boundaries.
- Snapshot tests verifying richer output at large sizes.

Success Criteria
- Same diagram looks richer on large screens.
- Small screens remain readable and non-overflowing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GreenCove","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:39:16.347554754Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:08:06.543604884Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:08:06.543587351Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u4fgt","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:42:20.143232230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u4fgt","depends_on_id":"bd-19cll","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:56:24.780123484Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u4fgt","depends_on_id":"bd-1oa1y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:48:24.192884549Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u4fgt","depends_on_id":"bd-25df9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:43:40.640554119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u4fgt","depends_on_id":"bd-2fp5r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:42:15.665513866Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u4fgt","depends_on_id":"bd-2oovu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:01.713147217Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u4fgt","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:00:01.941948451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":705,"issue_id":"bd-u4fgt","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scale adaptation complete in mermaid_render.rs. Added: select_fidelity() with density-based tier heuristics, RenderPlan struct, render_with_plan() respecting fidelity tiers (Rich/Normal/Compact/Outline), reserve_legend_area() for footnote regions, render_diagram_adaptive() convenience API. Explicit tier override via MermaidTier respected. Outline mode renders single-char nodes. Compact hides edge labels + clusters. 32 passing tests. All quality gates pass.","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:08:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ukp1f","title":"Sub-Cell Canvas Diagram Rendering","description":"## Goal
Render Mermaid diagrams using FrankenTUI's existing Canvas system (Braille 2×4, Block 2×2, HalfBlock 1×2) instead of cell-by-cell line-drawing characters. This gives 2-8x spatial resolution, enabling smooth diagonal edges, recognizable geometric shapes (real circles/diamonds/hexagons), proper arrowhead triangles, and filled regions.

## Why This Is Transformative
- The Canvas system ALREADY EXISTS (canvas.rs) with Painter, line/circle/rect/fill primitives, and 3 rendering modes.
- Currently the mermaid renderer writes line-drawing chars one cell at a time — max 1:1 pixel resolution.
- Sub-cell rendering turns diagrams from "ASCII art" to "terminal graphics" — a qualitative visual leap.
- Naturally solves shape rendering (circles become actual circles in Braille mode).
- Solves curved edges for free (Painter draws lines at arbitrary angles).
- Deterministic (no floating-point variance), fits FrankenTUI's correctness philosophy.

## Implementation Strategy
1. Add CanvasMermaidRenderer alongside existing cell-based MermaidRenderer in mermaid_render.rs.
2. Reuse Viewport transform (layout coords → canvas coords).
3. Use Painter for: filled rects (nodes), lines (edges), circles (round nodes), polygons (diamonds/hexagons), triangles (arrowheads).
4. Overlay text labels on the Buffer cell layer (Canvas + text compositing already works in canvas.rs).
5. Feature-gated behind "diagram" feature, rendering mode selectable via config.
6. Auto-detect best mode from TerminalCapabilities (Braille if unicode supported, Block/HalfBlock as fallbacks).

## Key Design Decisions
- Canvas rendering is an ALTERNATIVE to cell rendering, not a replacement. Both modes must work.
- Layout coordinates remain the same — only the rendering backend changes.
- Text labels always use cell-based rendering (you can't write text in Braille dots).
- Node borders + fills use Canvas; labels use Buffer cell writes — they composite naturally.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Diagrams render in Braille/Block/HalfBlock modes with visually recognizable shapes.
- Edges are smooth lines (not just orthogonal L-bends).
- Arrowheads are triangular, not just > characters.
- Node shapes (circle, diamond, hexagon, etc.) are geometrically correct.
- Text labels remain crisp and readable.
- Rendering mode auto-detected from terminal capabilities.
- All existing tests continue passing (cell mode unchanged).
- New snapshot tests for Canvas mode at 80×24 and 120×40.
- Performance: Canvas rendering within 2× of cell rendering time.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:25:54.880660705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:25:54.880660705Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering"]}
 {"id":"bd-ukp1f.1","title":"Canvas: rendering mode auto-detection + config","description":"## What
Auto-detect the best Canvas rendering mode from terminal capabilities, and provide config override.

## Mode Selection Logic
1. If terminal supports unicode + Braille characters → Braille mode (best: 2×4 resolution per cell)
2. If terminal supports unicode but not Braille → Block mode (2×2 resolution per cell)
3. If terminal supports half-block characters → HalfBlock mode (1×2 resolution per cell)
4. If ASCII only → fall back to cell-based rendering (existing MermaidRenderer)

## Config Override
- Add `render_mode` field to MermaidConfig: Auto | CellOnly | Braille | Block | HalfBlock
- Default: Auto (use terminal detection)
- MermaidShowcase demo: keybinding to cycle through modes (shows difference visually)

## Integration with GlyphPolicy
- Read from GlyphPolicy.unicode_box_drawing and GlyphPolicy.double_width to determine capabilities.
- If GlyphPolicy is ASCII mode, force CellOnly.

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid.rs (add render_mode to MermaidConfig)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (dispatch between Cell and Canvas renderers)
- crates/ftui-core/src/glyph_policy.rs (check Braille capability)

## Tests
- Unit: mode selection for each TerminalCapabilities profile (kitty→Braille, xterm→Block, vt100→CellOnly, dumb→CellOnly)
- Integration: render same diagram in all modes, verify no panics","notes":"Added unicode_braille capability flag in terminal_capabilities + capability_override; detection defaults to unicode_box_drawing. Pending integration into mermaid config/render once file reservations clear.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.550570767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:25:59.759588745Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:25:59.759569769Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.1","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.550570767Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-ukp1f.2","title":"Canvas: node shape rendering (all 8 shapes via Painter)","description":"## What
Implement Canvas-based rendering for all 8 NodeShape variants using Painter primitives from canvas.rs.

## Shape Mapping
- Rect → Painter::rect (filled rectangle with border)
- Rounded → Painter::rect with corner radius (approximate with arcs or short diagonals)
- Stadium → Painter::rect with semicircle ends (half-circles at left/right)
- Subroutine → Double-bordered rect (two nested rectangles)
- Diamond → Painter polygon: 4 vertices at (cx, top), (right, cy), (cx, bottom), (left, cy)
- Hexagon → Painter polygon: 6 vertices (flat-top or pointy-top orientation)
- Circle → Painter::circle centered on node, radius = min(width, height) / 2
- Asymmetric → Painter polygon: flag shape (rect with pointed right side)

## Implementation Details
- Each shape gets a dedicated fn: canvas_draw_rect, canvas_draw_diamond, canvas_draw_circle, etc.
- Fill interior with node's theme background color via Painter::fill
- Draw border in node's theme border color via Painter::line or Painter::polygon
- Scale from layout units to canvas pixel coordinates via Viewport transform
- Padding between border and label text must account for shape geometry (diamond has less usable interior than rect)

## Files to Modify
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (add canvas_render_node method)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs (if new primitives needed, e.g., polygon fill)

## Tests
- Unit test per shape: verify correct canvas pixels set for known input coordinates
- Snapshot tests: all 8 shapes rendered at 80×24 in Braille mode
- Property test: shape bounding box never exceeds allocated node rectangle","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.700733389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:16.823650122Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1emsq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:15.758468627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1qkni","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:16.823571945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.700733389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:13.660002036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Hexagon → Painter polygon: 6 vertices (flat-top or pointy-top orientation)
- Circle → Painter::circle centered on node, radius = min(width, height) / 2

## Implementation Details
- Each shape gets a dedicated fn: canvas_draw_rect, canvas_draw_diamond, canvas_draw_circle, etc.
- Fill interior with node's theme background color via Painter::fill
- Draw border in node's theme border color via Painter::line or Painter::polygon
- Scale from layout units to canvas pixel coordinates via Viewport transform

- Property test: shape bounding box never exceeds allocated node rectangle","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.700733389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:50:36.277134275Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1emsq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:15.758468627Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1qkni","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:16.823571945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.700733389Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:13.660002036Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ukp1f.3","title":"Canvas: text label compositing over canvas layer","description":"## What
Composite text labels (node labels, edge labels, cluster titles) on top of canvas-rendered shapes. Canvas draws geometry in sub-cell dots; text must be rendered in the cell layer on top.

## Implementation
- Canvas rendering writes to a CanvasLayer (Braille/Block/HalfBlock dots).
- Text rendering writes to the Buffer cell layer (standard Cell writes).
- When compositing: cells with text take priority over canvas dots in the same cell. This is how canvas.rs already works — Painter writes to a separate pixel grid that gets merged into Buffer cells, and explicit cell writes override.
- Center node labels within the shape's usable interior area (accounting for shape geometry — diamond has triangular corners, circle has curved edges).
- Edge labels: position at midpoint of edge, write text centered.
- Wrap long labels using existing wrap_text function.

## Key Design Decision
- Do NOT try to render text as Braille dots. Cell-based text is always more readable.
- Background color under text should match the node fill for readability.

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (add canvas_composite_labels)

## Tests
- Unit: label centered within various shapes at different sizes
- Snapshot: nodes with single-line and multi-line labels in Canvas mode","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.960464261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.445853490Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:52.960464261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.3","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.445816651Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ukp1f.4","title":"Canvas: snapshot tests + performance benchmarks","description":"## What
Comprehensive test suite for Canvas-mode diagram rendering.

## Snapshot Tests
- All 8 node shapes in Braille mode at 80×24
- All 8 node shapes in Block mode at 80×24
- 5-node flowchart in Braille at 80×24 and 120×40
- 10-node dense graph with crossing edges in Braille at 120×40
- Mixed shapes diagram (diamond decision + rect process + circle event) at 120×40
- Edge styles (solid, dashed, dotted, thick) in Braille mode
- Arrowhead types (normal, open, circle, diamond) in Braille mode

## Performance Tests
- Benchmark: 50-node graph render time in Braille vs Cell mode
- Assert: Canvas mode < 2× Cell mode render time
- Profile: identify any hot spots in Canvas ↔ Buffer compositing

## Determinism Tests
- Property test: same input + same mode = identical output (byte-for-byte)
- Cross-mode test: Canvas and Cell modes produce diagrams with same node positions (only visual fidelity differs)

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (tests module)
- crates/ftui-extras/tests/snapshots/ (new .snap files)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:53.286762256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:15.494821331Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:53.286762256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.713851018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:15.494780394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:15.238569856Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.4","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.976904184Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-ukp1f.5","title":"Canvas: triangular arrowhead rendering","description":"## What
Render arrowheads as filled triangles at edge endpoints using Painter polygon fill, instead of '>' / '<' / 'v' / '^' characters.

## Implementation
- Compute arrowhead direction from the last two waypoints of each edge.
- Triangle vertices: tip at endpoint, two base vertices perpendicular to edge direction, spread by configurable width (default 3-4 canvas pixels).
- Fill the triangle with the edge color.
- Support arrow types: normal (filled triangle), open (outline only), circle (small filled circle), cross (X mark), diamond (small filled diamond).

## Edge Cases
- Very short edges: skip arrowhead if edge length < arrowhead size.
- Bidirectional edges: arrowheads at both ends.
- Self-loops: arrowhead at the loop's return point.

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/mermaid_render.rs (add canvas_render_arrowhead)
- crates/ftui-extras/src/canvas.rs (add polygon_fill if not present)

## Tests
- Unit: arrowhead pointing in all 8 cardinal/ordinal directions
- Visual snapshot: edges with different arrow types","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:53.452291041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:49:50.676865167Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T16:49:50.676846292Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["canvas","mermaid","rendering"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:26:53.452291041Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-ukp1f.5","depends_on_id":"bd-ukp1f.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:30:14.184012018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
